output_text:
Summary: Reviewed the section and found one load-bearing internal contradiction plus a likely factual error about the benchmark protocol; several smaller issues listed after.

## Critical issues

**1. Per-node learned embeddings contradict the inductive claim.**
The section asserts SelectGNN is "fully inductive" and requires "no fine-tuning or re-optimization required when new nodes are introduced." It then says the model "learns a dedicated embedding vector e_v for each node v in the input graph, jointly with the GNN parameters," and concatenates e_v into the representation fed to both heads.

Per-node free-parameter embeddings are the defining feature of *transductive* methods (DeepWalk, node2vec, vanilla matrix-factorization-style GNN variants). For a node unseen at training time, e_v does not exist. Three options, all bad:
- Leave e_v undefined → the model cannot run on new nodes, directly falsifying the inductive claim.
- Plug in a zero/random/mean vector → the classifier and abstention heads were trained on *informative* e_v and now receive uninformative input; this is a train/test distribution shift that the reported numbers would not reflect unless test e_v was handled the same way.
- Optimize a new e_v at test time → that is fine-tuning, which the abstract explicitly disclaims.

The authors need to either (a) drop the per-node embedding, (b) replace it with something inductive (e.g., a function of node features), or (c) retract the "fully inductive / no re-optimization" claim and reframe as transductive (or semi-inductive with a documented cold-start procedure).

**2. The OGB-Arxiv / OGB-Products "standard inductive evaluation protocol" appears to be misdescribed.**
The standard OGB leaderboard protocols for `ogbn-arxiv` and `ogbn-products` are transductive: test nodes and their edges are present in the graph at training time; only their labels are withheld. The claim that test nodes "are unseen at training time" under the *standard* protocol is, to my knowledge, incorrect. There is a separate inductive variant of ogbn-products used in some papers (e.g., GraphSAINT-style splits) but it is not "the standard protocol." This needs a citation and the protocol described precisely — or the experiment needs to be rerun on a genuinely inductive split (e.g., PPI, or a held-out-subgraph split).

Note that issues (1) and (2) are mutually reinforcing: if the actual evaluation is transductive, the per-node embeddings work fine in the experiments, but then the inductive marketing in paragraph 1 is unsupported by the results.

## Smaller issues

- **Result reporting.** "Improves selective accuracy ... by 6.3%" — specify percentage points vs. relative, give variance over seeds, and report at multiple coverage levels (a single point on the risk-coverage curve is cherry-pickable). Only one of the two named benchmarks has a number.
- **Single baseline.** "GraphSAGE-Abstain" is the only comparator named. Standard selective-classification baselines (softmax-response thresholding, MC-dropout, Selective-Net, deep ensembles) are missing.
- **Coverage-risk objective is undefined.** "Minimizing a coverage-risk objective that trades off accuracy against abstention rate" is not a specification — give the loss, the trade-off coefficient, and how it was chosen.
- **Confounded abstention signal.** Because e_v is concatenated into the abstention head's input, the "structural ambiguity" interpretation in the last paragraph is unjustified — the head can abstain on the basis of node identity, not neighborhood structure. An ablation removing e_v from the abstention head (or from the whole model) is needed to support that causal attribution.
- **"L rounds of message passing"** — L is never specified.
- **Threshold tuning leakage risk.** "Tune tau on a held-out portion of the training graph" — confirm this split is disjoint from the evaluation set and from the validation set used for model selection.

## Uncertainty

The OGB-Arxiv/Products protocol claim (issue 2) is based on my recollection of the OGB benchmark conventions through ~2024; I did not re-check the current OGB documentation. The contradiction in issue 1 is a logical reading of the text and does not depend on external facts.
